category_title: Nonverbal students with ASD using iPads for communication and academic tasks
scenario_title: Repetitive stereotypical movements during instruction-following app
minimal_context: The student is using the Splingo's Language Universe app on an iPad in the classroom to place a virtual trashcan under a box.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time to use the Splingo's Language Universe app. Please place the trashcan under the box on the screen.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (The student sits with the iPad screen resting in an incorrect orientation.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's fix the screen. Now go ahead and try the activity.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (The student begins using repetitive and rapid swiping that is not part of the function of the app.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Look at the screen. Remember, you need to place the trashcan under the box.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (The trashcan is simply tapped repeatedly in a stereotypical manner instead of being moved.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time to use the Splingo's Language Universe app. Please place the trashcan under the box on the screen.

STUDENT: (The student sits with the iPad screen resting in an incorrect orientation.)

TUTOR: Let's fix the screen. Now go ahead and try the activity.

STUDENT: (The student begins using repetitive and rapid swiping that is not part of the function of the app.)

TUTOR: Look at the screen. Remember, you need to place the trashcan under the box.

STUDENT: (The trashcan is simply tapped repeatedly in a stereotypical manner instead of being moved.)
